metadata.description: 字符串: Python strings (str) are immutable sequences of Unicode characters. They support indexing, slicing, formatting, and a rich set of string methods. Here's a brief Python code example demonstrating some of these features: ```python # Creating a string my_string = "Hello, World!" # Indexing print(my_string[0]) # Output: H # Slicing print(my_string[7:]) # Output: World! # String formatting using f-strings name = "Alice" greeting = f"Hello, {name}!" print(greeting) # Output: Hello, Alice! ``` This example shows how to create a string, access characters by index, slice substrings, and format strings using f-strings.
